name: Banana Honey Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup uncooked Quaker oats (quick or old-fashioned)
recipeInstructions:
Pour honey into a medium-sized saucepan and bring to a boil. Add 1/2 teaspoon of baking soda, stir, and let stand for 10 minutes.
In a large bowl, beat butter until creamy. Gradually add sugar and beat until fluffy. Blend in eggs. Add the oats mixture and mashed bananas; blend well.
In another bowl, sift together flour, remaining 1/2 teaspoon baking soda, baking powder, and salt. Add to the creamed mixture and blend well.
Pour batter into two greased and waxed paper-lined 8-inch round cake pans. Bake in a preheated moderate oven (350°F) for 30-35 minutes. Cool on wire racks about 10 minutes before removing from pans and cooling completely.
For frosting, beat cream cheese until fluffy. Gradually add sifted confectioners' sugar and beat until smooth and of spreading consistency. Spread between layers and over the top of the cake. Refrigerate before serving. Makes one 2-layer cake.
Source: Our Favorites for family and friends
Reprinted with permission from The Quaker Oats Company
